prospect.Summary: Mushinski's squat frame belies his athleticism, and his powerful drop-and-drive delivery is a key component to his operation, as it creates upshot angle on his fastball that helps it play despite below-average velocity. His best secondary pitch is a plus curveball, and he also commands an average cutter to his glove side, which helps him garner weak contact. Though his curve and slider velocities overlap almost entirely, the two pitches have moderately different shapes, and he tends to use the curve against righties and the slider against lefties. At quick glance, he's easily an up/down down option, but Mushinski's command might enable him to be so consistent that he's just on the big league roster in perpetuity.

news.post_title: Projecting the College Players Taken on Day Two of the Draft

news.headline: Gets NRI deal from Rockies
news.news: The Rockies signed Mushinski to a minor-league contract with an invitation to spring training Monday, Kevin Henry of The Denver Gazette reports.
